laws.title: Public Law 15-104 | The Military Justice Reorganization and Reform Act
laws.final_statutory_text: Creates a chief judge advocate general, branch judge advocates general and deputies, military courts and judges, an ethics review board, confidentiality rules, and military paralegals for the armed forces.
laws.chronology: Source is intact and current; no later act in the reviewed set repeals or displaces it.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: No repeal, transfer, or redesignation chain was resolved; this is a standalone permanent military-justice reorganization.
laws.status_basis: Source intact and fully operative; the act establishes permanent military-justice offices, courts, confidentiality rules, and legal personnel. No self-repeal or sunset appears in the supplied materials.

current_implementation.detail: The current note declares 'Repealed or superseded' and carries a full-text quoted-content dump plus a Trello link. The source is current, so the note is misclassified and should not be pasted into permanent text.

recommended_actions: Remove the full-text quoted-content dump and the Trello link.
Correct the status from repealed or superseded to current.
Replace the boilerplate with a concise statutory note under title 10.

laws.title: Public Law 15-105 | The Municipal Prosecution Act of 2023
laws.final_statutory_text: Delegates District of Columbia municipal and federal prosecution to the Department of Justice and the U.S. Attorney, repeals PL 12-88 Part D and Title 6 section 37, and strikes and replaces specified provisions of PL 15-97.
laws.chronology: Source is intact and operative. The reviewed materials do not identify a later enactment that cleanly supersedes the act as a whole.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: The enactment is a bundle of direct repeal and substitution instructions affecting PL 12-88 and PL 15-97; no wholesale transfer or redesignation chain was resolved.
laws.status_basis: Source intact and operative; it makes multiple express repeals and amendments and delegates DC prosecution authority to DOJ. Nothing in the supplied set repeals PL 15-105.

current_implementation.detail: The XML note is boilerplate, declares the law repealed or superseded, and fails to record the express repeals and amendments to PL 12-88 and PL 15-97. Placement at title 40 is odd for a DC-prosecution/judiciary act; title 28 fits better. Trello link present; no dump.

recommended_actions: Implement the amendments to PL 15-97 and repeal-mark PL 12-88 Part D and Title 6 section 37.
Correct the status to current and re-anchor to title 28.
Remove the Trello link.

laws.title: Public Law 15-106 | The Congress Strikes Back Repealment
laws.exact_target: The Congress Strikes Back Act of 2022
laws.final_statutory_text: Repeals The Congress Strikes Back Act of 2022 in its entirety.
laws.chronology: Current repealing statute. Its only operative effect is the repeal of the earlier act, and no later material in the reviewed set displaces that repeal.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: Pure repeal of a non-Code special act; no transfer or redesignation chain and no new permanent Code text is needed.
laws.status_basis: Source intact and operative; its sole substantive act is the express repeal of The Congress Strikes Back Act of 2022. Nothing in the supplied set repeals PL 15-106 itself.

current_implementation.detail: The note declares 'Repealed or superseded' about PL 15-106 itself, but this repealing act is current. The actual repeal of The Congress Strikes Back Act is not implemented.

recommended_actions: Repeal-mark The Congress Strikes Back Act at its recorded location and add an amendment note citing PL 15-106 section III.
Correct the status to current.
Remove the Trello link.
Keep the act as a statutory note under title 2.

laws.title: Public Law 15-108 | The Reclassification of Labeling Act of 2023
laws.final_statutory_text: Sets congressional labeling conventions for bills, resolutions, concurrent resolutions, joint resolutions, amendments, committee reports, and treaty documents; it is an internal procedural enactment rather than a freestanding positive-law section.
laws.chronology: Source is intact and operative. No later enactment in the reviewed set supersedes it, but the subject matter is procedural and non-Code.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: No repeal or transfer issue; the enactment belongs, if anywhere, as a statutory note or chamber-rule-style provision rather than a new Code section.
laws.status_basis: Source intact and operative. It regulates congressional document labeling and does not create a positive-law USC section.

current_implementation.detail: The XML note is anchored at title 50, which is wrong for a congressional-labeling act. Title 2 fits better. Declared current is right, but the codification prose is boilerplate and no actual Code amendment is made. Trello link present; no dump.

recommended_actions: Re-anchor from title 50 to title 2 as a statutory or historical note.
Do not treat the procedural amendment language as USC amendments.
Remove the Trello link.
Keep it as a note only if the repository wants internal congressional procedure material.

laws.title: Public Law 15-109 | Ending Tigray for the Last Time
laws.final_statutory_text: Formally repeals House Joint Resolution 6 and ends the Tigray war notice.
laws.chronology: Current and operative as a repeal instrument; no later enactment in the reviewed materials displaces the repeal effect.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: Straight repeal of a joint resolution; no permanent Code section is needed.
laws.status_basis: Source intact and operative as a joint resolution. Its sole act is to repeal House Joint Resolution 6, so it is a current repealing instrument, not a permanent Code section.

current_implementation.detail: The note declares 'Repealed or superseded' about PL 15-109 itself, which is wrong because this resolution is the repealing instrument and is current. The actual action - marking HJRes 6 repealed - is not done. The title 2 anchor is generic; a war-powers or foreign-relations anchor fits better. Trello link present; no dump.

recommended_actions: Repeal-mark House Joint Resolution 6 wherever it is recorded and add a note citing PL 15-109.
Correct the status of PL 15-109 to current.
Exclude PL 15-109 from permanent Code text and keep only a concise historical note.
Remove the Trello link.

laws.title: Public Law 15-111 | Presidential Transition Protection Act
laws.final_statutory_text: Defines presidential-transition terms, prohibits obstruction of a presidential transition, sets a five-to-seven-day jail term and/or up to a $1,000 fine, and bars no future-office disqualification.
laws.chronology: Source is intact and operative. No later enactment in the reviewed set supersedes it.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: No repeal or transfer chain; this is a standalone permanent criminal-prohibition measure that belongs as a note in Title 18, with the transition definitions potentially anchored in Title 3.
laws.status_basis: Source is intact and operative. It creates a criminal prohibition on obstructing a presidential transition, adds definitions, and includes severability and effective-date language.

current_implementation.detail: The note is placed under title 49, which is the wrong subject matter for a presidential-transition criminal-offense statute. Title 3 or title 18 fits better. The note is otherwise a boilerplate statutory note and includes a Trello link.

recommended_actions: Re-anchor from title 49 to title 3 (presidential transition) or title 18 (criminal offense), preferably title 18 for SEC III.
Replace the boilerplate note with a concise statutory note capturing the transition prohibition, definitions, and penalty.
Remove the Trello link.

laws.title: Public Law 16-113 | The Bing Bong Act of 2023
laws.final_statutory_text: Repeals Public Law 15-96 in its entirety.
laws.chronology: Current repealing statute. Its operative effect is to repeal PL 15-96, which is fully repealed in the reviewed materials.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: Complete repeal of PL 15-96; no surviving transfer or redesignation requires code text.
laws.status_basis: Source is intact and operative, but its sole substantive effect is to repeal PL 15-96 in full. That makes PL 16-113 itself a current repealing statute while PL 15-96 is fully repealed.

current_implementation.detail: The current note labels the repealing act itself as repealed or superseded and leaves the repeal of PL 15-96 unimplemented. Title 18 is a better anchor than title 50.

recommended_actions: Apply repeal-marking to PL 15-96 wherever it is codified.
Replace the boilerplate note with a concise note describing PL 16-113 as a current repealing statute.
Remove the Trello link.
